revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24432061637|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:祇園神社 (倉敷市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Gion Shinto shrine main shrine of Shimotsui.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kojima Area|qid=Q11388647|1=Kojima Area}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.43709315680419|N|133.79499410362487|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Gion Jinja (祇園神社) |image = [[File:Gion Shinto shrine main shrine of Shimotsui.JPG|250px]] |Location = {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}} {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City|1=Kurashiki}} {{ill|Shimotsui (Kurashiki City)|de|GermanName|ja|下津井 (倉敷市)|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui (Kurashiki City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}1-13-16 |Coordinates = |Deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|GermanName|ja|素戔嗚命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Shrine Rank = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) |Established = Unknown |Honden = |Alias = Gion-san |Fudasyo = |Annual festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday in July |Rites = Nagahama Festival (4th Saturday and Sunday in October) }}
'''Gion Shrine''' (祇園神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the {{ill|Kojima area (Kurashiki City)|de|GermanName|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|lt=Kojima area|lt_ja=児島地域|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima area (Kurashiki City)|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11388647}} of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is also called '''Shimotsui Gion Jinja''' (下津井祇園神社).
== Deities == They enshrine {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|de|GermanName|ja|素戔嗚尊|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素戔嗚尊|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Kushinada-hime|de|GermanName|ja|奇稲田姫命|lt=Kushinada-hime|lt_ja=奇稲田姫命|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinada-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}}, {{ill|Orochi no Aramasa|de|GermanName|ja|蛇の麁正|lt=Ōrochi no Aramasa|lt_ja=大蛇麁正（おろちのあらまさ）|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi no Aramasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nagahama-no-Kami, Hassha Daimyōjin, and {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|de|GermanName|ja|事代主神|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|lt_ja=事代主神|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8009611}}. They are believed to bring blessings for maritime safety and protection against epidemics.
== History == [[File:The Inland Sea is faced from the Gion Shinto shrine.JPG|thumb|right|250px|A view of the Seto Inland Sea from the shrine precinct]] '''Gion Shrine''' is the ujigami (tutelary deity) of the {{ill|Shimotsui district|de|GermanName|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui district|lt_ja=下津井地区|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui district|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361095}}, affectionately referred to as “Gion-san.” It is enshrined on top of Mount Jōyama (浄山), which juts out over the sea at an elevation of 22 meters. When it was merged with the Gion-gū, two connected main sanctuaries were formed. In addition, the present approach to the shrine was donated by the {{ill|Ikeda clan (Mino Ikeda branch)|de|GermanName|ja|池田氏#美濃池田家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan (Mino Ikeda branch)|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2975277}} in the 3rd year of {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|1863|en|1863|qid=Q7701|lt=1863|1=1863}}). In former times, there was a thriving ryotei (traditional restaurant) called “Kinparō” at the foot of the hill, but it was burned down in an arson fire in June of Heisei 8 (1996).
The year of Gion Jinja’s founding is unknown. Its precincts originally corresponded to the ruins of the old Nagahama Castle, which was built around the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. In the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it was founded as the guardian shrine of that castle, known then as “Nagahama-gū” or “Nagahama Daimyōjin.” Around the latter half of the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was merged with the Gion-gū and became “Gion Jinja.” In modern times, it was renamed “Susanoo Jinja” in December of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}}), but then returned to the name “Gion Jinja” in September of Meiji 21 ({{ill|1888|en|1888|qid=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}}). Later, in Meiji 43 ({{ill|1910|en|1910|qid=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}}), “Hachihashira Jinja” was also merged here.
Since ancient times, this location was the scene of numerous battles. During the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|qid=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}}, it is said {{ill|Taira clan|de|GermanName|ja|平家|lt=Taira clan|lt_ja=平家|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏}} forces used it as a stronghold in preparation for the {{ill|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|en|Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|qid=Q2116658|lt=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|1=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani}} on the {{ill|Honshu|en|Honshu|qid=Q13989|lt=Honshū|1=Honshu}} side. After a contingent of the Taira had crossed over from {{ill|Shikoku|en|Shikoku|qid=Q13991|lt=Shikoku|1=Shikoku}}, a group of Shikoku warriors switched allegiance to the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|qid=Q599881|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Minamoto clan}} and sailed with around a dozen warships to attack the waters off Shimotsui. Because the Taira generals such as {{ill|Taira no Norimori|en|Taira no Norimori|qid=Q11060997|lt=Taira no Norimori|1=Taira no Norimori}}, {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|de|GermanName|ja|平通盛|lt=Taira no Tomomori|lt_ja=平通盛|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Tomomori|1=Taira no Tomomori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483584}}, and {{ill|Taira no Noritsune|en|Taira no Noritsune|qid=Q1189315|lt=Taira no Kiyotsune|1=Taira no Noritsune}} were entrenched here, the attackers were easily driven back to Shikoku. Later, during the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, it is recorded that {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, leading more than a thousand warships from {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|qid=Q13987|lt=Kyūshū|1=Kyushu}}, stayed in this area for several days en route to the east.
== Festivals == * 3rd Saturday and Sunday in July: Gion Festival * 4th Saturday and Sunday in October: Nagahama Festival
== Sacred Grove == Because the shrine precinct is situated on a small promontory above the {{ill|Seto Inland Sea|en|Seto Inland Sea|qid=Q231312|lt=Seto Inland Sea|1=Seto Inland Sea}}, the surrounding grove (the chinju no mori) features many coastal plant species. In particular, the grove is notable for large stands of {{ill|Quercus phillyreoides|en|Quercus phillyreoides|qid=Q10877943|lt=Ubamegashi|1=Quercus phillyreoides}}, known for producing binchōtan charcoal. There is also the broadleaf evergreen {{ill|Cleyera japonica var. grandis|de|GermanName|ja|クスドイゲ|lt=Kusudo-ige|lt_ja=クスドイゲ|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cleyera japonica var. grandis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Kusudo-ige}}, which is rare in this region and of academic value. Formerly, a unique weeping cherry tree (Gion-shidare) stood in front of the main shrine, but it has since died. Altogether, around 30 species (including both wild and planted) compose this shrine forest, which was designated an Okayama Prefectural Folk Cultural Property in Showa 56 (1981).<ref>[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/seikatsu/sizen/sizenkouen.etc/area/417shimotsui.html 岡山県環境文化部自然環境課、「下津井祇園神社の社叢」（おかやまの自然公園）].</ref>
== Stored Materials == “Gion Documents” (Gion Monjo), records from the late Edo period relating to this shrine, are designated Important Cultural Properties of Kurashiki City. They include information on business practices of the time and donations made to the shrine, reflecting the economic prosperity of Shimotsui.<ref>[http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/dd.aspx?menuid=5481 倉敷市役所、「下津井祇園文書」].</ref> Additionally, the model of the {{ill|Gozabune|de|GermanName|ja|御座船|lt=御za-bune|lt_ja=御座船|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozabune|1=gozabune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489006}} “Hakō-maru,” the official boat of the {{ill|Bizen Domain|de|GermanName|ja|備前藩|lt=Bizen Domain|lt_ja=備前藩|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岡山藩|1=Okayama Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q903497}}, was donated in {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=1811 (Bunka 8)|1=Bunka}} by {{ill|Ikeda Narimasa|de|GermanName|ja|池田斉政|lt=Ikeda Narimasa|lt_ja=池田斉政|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Narimasa|1=Ikeda Narimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11551949}} to the Nagahama-gū and Gion-gū shrines in Shimotsui. Built at a 1/10 scale, the entire hull is finished in lacquer with patches of {{ill|Gold leaf|en|Gold leaf|qid=Q929186|lt=gold leaf|1=Gold leaf}} and decorative metal fittings. In the past, this model was carried on a {{ill|Danjiri|de|GermanName|ja|だんじり|lt=danjiri|lt_ja=だんじり|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11271630}} float during festivals, but it is now preserved in the shrine’s storage facility.<ref>[http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/dd.aspx?menuid=5467 倉敷市役所、「御座船模型」].</ref>
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== Access == * From {{ill|Kojima Station|en|Kojima Station|qid=Q4981057|lt=Kojima Station|1=Kojima Station}}, take the {{ill|Shimotsui Dentetsu|de|GermanName|ja|下津井電鉄|lt=Shimotsui Dentetsu (Bus)|lt_ja=下津井電鉄|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui Dentetsu|1=Shimotsui Dentetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361100}} bus (Shimotsui Loop Bus “Tokohaigo”) and get off at the Gion Jinja-shita stop.
== External Links == * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/17146/ Gion Jinja] (Okayama Prefecture Jinja-chō) {{ill|Template:Shinto Yokobox|de|GermanName|ja|神道 横|lt=Shinto Yokobox|lt_ja=神道 横|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Template:Shinto Yokobox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Template:Shinto-stub|de|GermanName|ja|Shinto-stub|lt=Shinto-stub|lt_ja=Shinto-stub|lt_de=GermanName|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Template:Shinto-stub|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} -->
{{translated page|ja|祇園神社 (倉敷市)|version=100621508|comment=Imported full ja history}}
